title: Galaxy S26 Ultra Powers ‘#PlayGalaxy Cup: PUBG Mobile Global Open’
content: ▲ Players lift the championship trophy in celebration. (From left) Sunny, CouRage, NiceWigg and Octane On February 26, Samsung Electronics hosted the #PlayGalaxy Cup: PUBG Mobile Global Open in San Francisco, marking the kickoff of the 2026 #PlayGalaxy Cup annual league. The event drew strong interest from mobile esports fans worldwide and signaled the start of a new global competitive season. The #PlayGalaxy Cup is a global tournament league highlighting the gaming performance of Galaxy devices. In 2026, the tournament expands into a yearlong global esports league in collaboration with PUBG Mobile — shifting from standalone events to a full-season format. ▲ Player vonnyfelicia focuses on her gameplay The Global Open served as a showcase match celebrating the league’s launch. Sunny, CouRage, NiceWigg and Octane of North American gaming organization 100 Thieves competed alongside OfflineTV members TinaKitten, Yvonnie, Foolish and Masayoshi. They were joined by eight members of TeamGalaxy — a global roster of influencers — forming a 16-player lineup of creators and competitors. Players competed using the new Galaxy S26 Ultra, demonstrating that immersive, high-performance esports gameplay on a mobile device. Cheers erupted throughout each round, reflecting the excitement inside the venue. Streamer Ludwig hosted the event, bringing live interviews and onstage commentary throughout the competition. Between matches, he engaged in spontaneous conversations with players and interacted with fans in real time, adding to the festival atmosphere. The #PlayGalaxy Cup: PUBG Mobile Global Open was livestreamed on Ludwig’s YouTube channel, drawing 16.77 million views and broad attention from the gaming community. The broadcast was also streamed on Samsung’s YouTube channel and co-streamed by creators including Ashtax and Wynnsanity. The hybrid format — engaging both on-site spectators and online viewers — underscored the league’s vision of a globally accessible mobile esports competition. Fans at the event rooted for their favorite teams. A large screen at center stage displayed smooth, immersive gameplay on Galaxy S26 Ultra and a dedicated experience zone allowed visitors to test the device’s gaming performance. Following the tournament, #PlayGalaxy Cup moves into its full annual league schedule. Beginning in May, regional qualifiers will be held across Europe, Southeast Asia and Oceania, North America, India and South America. Additional details will be shared through each region’s host streamer channels, and all matches will be livestreamed globally. Top teams will advance to the World Final at Gamescom 2026 in Cologne, Germany — one of the world’s largest gaming events — to compete for the championship. “The Global Open marked a milestone with the launch of the inaugural 2026 #PlayGalaxy Cup league,” said David Moon, Head of Influencer Marketing, Mobile eXperience (MX) Business at Samsung Electronics. “We look forward to continuing this journey with gaming fans around the world.”

title: [Video] Everyday Freedom, Hands-Free With the Galaxy Buds4 Series
content: Reaching for a phone while cooking or exercising — even with wireless earbuds on — can be inconvenient. Samsung Electronics’ new Galaxy Buds4 series combines immersive sound with an ergonomic design to deliver a truly hands-free experience powered by AI. Users can answer calls with a nod or activate AI agents by voice, accessing a range of features without ever taking out their phones. Enhanced Adaptive Active Noise Cancellation (ANC) analyzes the wearing conditions in real time for optimal noise control, with seamless connectivity across the Galaxy ecosystem. Samsung Newsroom takes a closer look at the everyday freedom enabled by the Galaxy Buds4 series in the video below.

title: [Video] Four Ways the Galaxy S26 Series Expands What’s Possible
content: Anyone who has ever wanted to shield a phone screen on a crowded subway, have someone else answer an unfamiliar call or create a personal, one-of-a-kind sticker — that moment has arrived. Samsung Electronics’ third-generation AI phone brings those possibilities to life. Powered by true agentic AI that understands context and anticipates user needs, the Galaxy S26 series makes everyday experiences more intuitive and convenient. Samsung Newsroom highlights the key features that expand what’s possible with the Galaxy S26 series in the videos below. Privacy Display: Built-In Screen Protection Agentic AI: Context-Aware, Proactive Assistance Camera and Editing: Smarter With AI Creative Studio: Personalized Content Creation

title: Galaxy S26 Ultra Brings Chile’s Unmapped Landscapes to Google Street View
content: Samsung Electronics has launched “Chile Expanding the Map,” an initiative designed to showcase remote and lesser-known locations across Chile that do not appear on digital maps. The project encourages participants to explore hard-to-access areas and capture them using the advanced camera capabilities of Galaxy S26 Ultra. The images are then uploaded to Google Street View, expanding virtual access to landscapes that have remained largely invisible online. San Pedro de Atacama marked the starting point of the initiative. Known as one of Chile’s most iconic and photographed destinations, the region provided a striking backdrop for the first explorations. Through curated desert experiences, participants captured landscapes using a range of photography and video techniques, focusing on locations and angles that are often overlooked by tourists. Images from these experiences highlight the unique textures and colors of the Atacama Desert, emphasizing how Galaxy S26 Ultra captures detailed nighttime photos with its enhanced Nightography and expansive landscape shots. By contributing these visuals to Google Street View, the initiative has begun building a richer and more inclusive digital representation of Chile’s geography. San Pedro de Atacama represents only the beginning. “Chile Expanding the Map” is set to expand to other regions, gradually creating a visual route that documents landscapes still missing from digital platforms. Through this growing archive, Samsung aims to redefine how people explore Chile — demonstrating that some of the country’s most extraordinary destinations are those that have yet to appear on the map. Learn more at the Samsung Chile Newsroom
